description: A striking houseplant whose patterned leaves fold upward at night like praying hands.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium evenly moist; water when the top 1-2 cm feels slightly dry, about every 3 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; direct sun fades the leaf patterns.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim browning leaf tips and old leaves to encourage bushy new growth.
careDetails.commonProblems: Crispy brown leaf edges from low humidity or hard tap water.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (expanded clay) — holds moisture without waterlogging
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — lush new leaves visible within 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: Maranta loves humidity, so semi-hydro suits it well. Use distilled or rainwater to avoid leaf-tip burn and keep only the lower roots wet.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs (ASPCA-listed non-toxic)

name: Nerve Plant
description: A compact tropical plant prized for leaves laced with vivid white, pink, or red veins.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist; it wilts dramatically but recovers fast after watering, roughly every 2 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light or filtered shade; avoid direct sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted liquid fertilizer once a month while actively growing.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back stem tips regularly to keep the plant dense and prevent legginess.
careDetails.commonProblems: Sudden wilting and leaf drop from dry soil or low humidity.

hidroponik.media: Fine hydroton or perlite for steady capillary moisture
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — full bushy growth within 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: Because Fittonia hates drying out, a wicking semi-hydro setup keeps moisture constant. Cover the surface to raise humidity around the leaves.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs (ASPCA-listed non-toxic)

name: Dieffenbachia
description: A bold foliage plant with large green leaves splashed in creamy white and yellow.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2-3 cm of soil is dry, about every 4 days; avoid soggy roots.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates medium shade but grows leggy in deep shade.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Cut back the leggy top to force bushier growth; wear gloves and wash hands after.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellowing lower leaves from overwatering or cold drafts.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (expanded clay) in a dark container
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new leaves unfurl within 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: Root a stem cutting in plain water for 2-3 weeks before moving to nutrients. The sap is irritating, so handle cuttings with gloves.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral pain, drooling, swelling, vomiting if chewed

name: Umbrella Plant (Schefflera)
description: A fast-growing shrub with glossy leaflets arranged like the spokes of an umbrella.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2-3 cm of soil is dry, roughly every 5 days; let excess drain away.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light keeps it full; low light causes sparse, leggy stems.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim tall stems to encourage branching and a compact, bushy shape.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf drop from overwatering, drafts, or sudden light changes; prone to spider mites.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — vigorous new shoots within 1-2 months
hidroponik.tips: Schefflera adapts easily to water culture. Rinse soil off the roots gently before transferring and refresh the solution every 2 weeks.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, drooling, vomiting, difficulty swallowing

name: Ponytail Palm
description: A drought-tough plant with a swollen water-storing trunk and a fountain of curling leaves.

careDetails.watering: Water sparingly; let the soil dry out fully, about every 2 weeks. The bulbous base stores water.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light to full sun; it thrives on a sunny windowsill.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light cactus or balanced fertilizer every 2 months in spring and summer only.
careDetails.pruning: Only trim brown or broken leaf tips; it needs little pruning.
careDetails.commonProblems: Soft, rotting base from overwatering — the most common way to kill it.

hidroponik.tips: As a drought-adapted succulent-like plant, the ponytail palm needs its caudex to dry between waterings and rots in constant moisture, so it is unsuitable for hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs (ASPCA-listed non-toxic)

name: Kalanchoe
description: A succulent with thick scalloped leaves and long-lasting clusters of bright flowers.

careDetails.watering: Let the soil dry between waterings, about weekly; reduce in winter to prevent rot.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light to several hours of direct sun encourages abundant blooming.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced or bloom-boosting liquid fertilizer once a month during flowering.
careDetails.pruning: Deadhead spent flower clusters to tidy the plant and encourage reblooming.
careDetails.commonProblems: Stem and root rot from overwatering; soft, mushy leaves signal too much water.

hidroponik.tips: As a succulent that stores water in its leaves and rots easily in standing water, Kalanchoe needs a dry, well-draining medium and is not suited to hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Bufadienolides — vomiting, diarrhea, and rarely abnormal heart rhythm

name: Christmas Cactus
description: A forest cactus with flattened, segmented stems that bursts into bright pink or red blooms around the holiday season.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2-3 cm of soil feels dry, roughly weekly; reduce slightly after flowering.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid harsh midday sun which scorches the segments.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply diluted houseplant fertilizer monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch off a few segments after blooming to encourage bushier branching.
careDetails.commonProblems: Bud drop from sudden temperature changes; stem rot from overwatering.

hidroponik.tips: As a succulent forest cactus, its stems rot easily in constant water, so it is not suited to hydroponic systems and prefers a fast-draining soil mix.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA

name: Yucca
description: An architectural, drought-tolerant plant with woody canes topped by rosettes of stiff, sword-shaped leaves.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply only when the soil is largely dry, about every 10-14 days; far less in winter.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light, including several hours of direct sun; tolerates a sunny window.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed lightly with balanced fertilizer every 2 months in spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Remove dried lower leaves; cut the cane to control height if it grows too tall.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering; leaf-tip browning in very dry air.

hidroponik.tips: Yucca has a thick woody trunk and drought-adapted roots that rot in standing water, so it is unsuitable for hydroponics and needs a gritty, fast-draining soil.

toxicity.note: Saponins are toxic to pets, causing vomiting and drooling

name: Air Plant
description: A tiny rootless epiphyte that absorbs moisture through its silvery leaves and blushes red when about to bloom.

careDetails.watering: Soak in water for 20-30 minutes about twice a week, then shake out and dry fully.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; protect from direct midday sun that dries it out.
careDetails.fertilizer: Mist with a very dilute bromeliad fertilizer about once a month.
careDetails.pruning: Trim off any brown leaf tips with scissors; remove spent flowers.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rot at the base if left wet; dehydration and curling if under-soaked.

hidroponik.tips: Air plants have no functional roots and absorb water through their leaves; sitting in a nutrient solution would rot the base, so they cannot be grown hydroponically.

toxicity.note: Tillandsia air plants are non-toxic to cats and dogs

name: Peperomia
description: A compact houseplant with thick, glossy, spoon-shaped leaves that stores water and tolerates neglect well.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top half of the soil is dry, roughly weekly; its fleshy leaves hold reserves.
careDetails.sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light; tolerates lower light but grows leggy.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply diluted balanced fertilizer monthly during spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch growing tips to keep the plant bushy and compact.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot and limp leaves from overwatering; leaf drop from cold drafts.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — steady leaf growth visible within 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: Peperomia roots easily in water; start with a stem cutting in clean water for 1-2 weeks before moving it into a dilute nutrient solution and keep only the lower third of the roots submerged.

toxicity.note: Peperomia obtusifolia is non-toxic to cats and dogs

name: Norfolk Island Pine
description: A graceful indoor conifer with soft, symmetrical tiers of needled branches, often grown as a living tabletop tree.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil lightly moist; water when the top 2-3 cm is dry, about weekly.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light with some direct morning sun; rotate for even growth.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply diluted balanced fertilizer monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Avoid heavy pruning; only remove dead lower branches as they brown.
careDetails.commonProblems: Browning, dropping needles from dry air or letting the soil fully dry out.

hidroponik.tips: As a slow-growing conifer with a deep woody root system, it needs a stable airy soil mix and does not adapt to hydroponic culture.

toxicity.note: Mildly toxic; sap and needles may cause vomiting and irritation

name: String of Hearts
description: A trailing succulent vine with delicate heart-shaped, marbled leaves cascading on thin purple stems.

careDetails.watering: Let the soil dry out almost completely, then water thoroughly, roughly every 10-14 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; some gentle direct sun deepens the leaf marbling.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed lightly with diluted succulent fertilizer every 2 months in the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim leggy strands to encourage fuller growth; cuttings root easily.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mushy stems and rot from overwatering; sparse growth in too little light.

hidroponik.tips: This drought-adapted succulent stores water in its tubers and leaves and rots quickly in standing water, so it is not suited to hydroponics and prefers a gritty cactus mix.

toxicity.note: String of Hearts is non-toxic to cats and dogs

name: Parsley
description: A biennial culinary herb with bright, curly or flat leaves used fresh as a garnish and seasoning.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist; water every 1-2 days, never letting it dry out completely.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade, about 6 hours of light per day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with balanced liquid fertilizer every 3 weeks for lush leaves.
careDetails.pruning: Harvest outer stems at the base regularly to encourage new growth.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellowing leaves from overwatering; aphids and leaf spot in humid conditions.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: First harvest in 70-90 days; cut outer leaves continuously
hidroponik.tips: Germination is slow (2-3 weeks); soak seeds overnight first. Keep the solution cool to prevent bolting.

toxicity.note: Spring parsley phototoxic; large amounts cause GI upset in pets

name: Dill
description: A feathery aromatic annual herb prized for its delicate fronds and seeds in pickles and fish dishes.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days to keep soil evenly moist but not soggy.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6-8 hours daily for the best flavor.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light feeding with balanced fertilizer every 3 weeks; avoid excess nitrogen.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch growing tips to delay flowering and keep foliage bushy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Bolts quickly in heat; prone to aphids and powdery mildew.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Leaves ready in 40-60 days; harvest fronds before flowering
hidroponik.tips: Provide support as the tall stems grow. Harvest often and keep solution cool to slow bolting.

toxicity.note: Considered non-toxic to dogs, cats, and horses

name: Sage
description: A hardy woody perennial with soft, grey-green aromatic leaves used in savory cooking.

careDetails.watering: Let the top of the soil dry between waterings; water every 3-4 days as it dislikes wet roots.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, 6-8 hours daily, for compact aromatic growth.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light monthly feeding; sage thrives in lean soil and needs little fertilizer.
careDetails.pruning: Prune lightly after flowering and trim woody stems in spring to keep it bushy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering; powdery mildew in humid, crowded conditions.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: First leaves in 60-80 days; harvest steadily once established
hidroponik.tips: Keep humidity moderate and ensure good airflow to prevent mildew. Avoid over-rich nutrient solution.

toxicity.note: Common sage is non-toxic to cats, dogs, and horses

name: Chives
description: A clumping perennial herb with slender hollow leaves and a mild onion flavor, easy to grow.

careDetails.watering: Keep soil consistently moist; water every 1-2 days during active growth.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to light shade, about 6 hours of light per day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with balanced liquid fertilizer every 3 weeks for continuous leaves.
careDetails.pruning: Snip leaves to about 5 cm above soil; regular cutting boosts regrowth.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellow tips from inconsistent watering; thrips and rust occasionally.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: First cutting in 50-70 days; harvest repeatedly every few weeks
hidroponik.tips: Sow several seeds per cube for a dense clump. Cut regularly to keep leaves tender and productive.

toxicity.note: Allium toxin damages red blood cells; harmful to cats and dogs

name: Marjoram
description: A tender aromatic perennial related to oregano, with small soft leaves and a sweet, mild flavor.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; let the top of the soil dry slightly between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, 6-8 hours daily, for the strongest aroma.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light feeding every month; marjoram prefers lean, well-drained soil.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch tips regularly to encourage bushy growth and prevent flowering.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering; spider mites in dry indoor air.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: First harvest in 50-70 days; clip sprigs as needed afterward
hidroponik.tips: Ensure good airflow and avoid an over-rich solution. Pinch often to keep plants compact and aromatic.

toxicity.note: Marjoram is considered non-toxic to dogs and cats

name: Fennel
description: A tall feathery perennial herb with an anise-like flavor, grown for its leaves, seeds, and bulb.

careDetails.watering: Keep soil evenly moist; water every 1-2 days, especially while bulbs form.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6-8 hours daily, for vigorous growth.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with balanced fertilizer every 3 weeks during active growth.
careDetails.pruning: Remove flower stalks to prolong leaf and bulb production.
careDetails.commonProblems: Bolts in heat; aphids cluster on tender new shoots.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Leaves in 60-80 days; bulbs need more space and 80-100 days
hidroponik.tips: Give it ample vertical space and support for tall stems. Grow apart from dill to avoid cross-pollination.

toxicity.note: Fennel is generally non-toxic to dogs and cats

name: Kale
description: A hardy, nutrient-dense leafy green with curly blue-green leaves, rich in vitamins K, A, and C.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium consistently moist; water once a day, especially in dry weather.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade, at least 6 hours of light a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply nitrogen-rich liquid fertilizer every 2 weeks for lush leaves.
careDetails.pruning: Harvest the outer leaves first so the central crown keeps producing.
careDetails.commonProblems: Cabbage worms, aphids, and yellowing leaves from nitrogen shortage.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 50–65 days after transplanting; pick outer leaves continuously.
hidroponik.tips: Sow in rockwool for 7–10 days first, then transplant. Kale thrives in cooler conditions, so keep the solution from overheating.

toxicity.note: Brassica safe in tiny amounts; excess causes gas, thyroid issues

name: Broccoli
description: A cool-season vegetable grown for its dense green flower heads, packed with fiber and antioxidants.

careDetails.watering: Water daily to keep the soil evenly moist; uneven watering splits the heads.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours, but prefers cooler temperatures.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with balanced NPK every 2 weeks, boosting nitrogen early on.
careDetails.pruning: Cut the main head before flowers open; side shoots keep producing after.
careDetails.commonProblems: Cabbage worms, aphids, and premature flowering (bolting) in heat.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Vegetable AB Mix 1400–1800 ppm (EC 2.8–3.5) — heavy feeder
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 70–90 days from transplant; cut the central head, then harvest side shoots.
hidroponik.tips: Broccoli is a heavy feeder needing strong aeration and cool roots. Keep the solution below 22°C to prevent bolting in warm climates.

toxicity.note: Brassica safe in tiny amounts; excess causes gas, thyroid issues

name: Radish
description: A fast-growing root crop with crisp, peppery bulbs ready to harvest in just a few weeks.

careDetails.watering: Water daily and evenly; dry spells make the roots woody and overly hot.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours a day for good root formation.
careDetails.fertilizer: Use low-nitrogen, higher-potassium fertilizer so energy goes to the root.
careDetails.pruning: Thin seedlings to 3–5 cm apart so each bulb has room to swell.
careDetails.commonProblems: Flea beetles, root cracking, and all-leaf growth from excess nitrogen.

hidroponik.tips: Radish is a root crop that needs a deep, loose medium for its bulb to swell. Standard water-based hydroponic systems do not support proper root development.

toxicity.note: Generally safe; excess may cause gas and stomach upset

name: Beetroot
description: A sweet, deep-red root vegetable whose leaves are also edible, rich in folate and iron.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist; water daily to avoid tough, fibrous roots.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to light shade, at least 6 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced fertilizer with extra potassium and boron for sweet roots.
careDetails.pruning: Thin seedlings to 8–10 cm apart; young leaves can be picked for salad.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf miners, boron-deficiency black spots, and crowded, stunted roots.

hidroponik.tips: Beetroot is a root crop that needs a deep, loose medium for its bulb to develop. Standard water-based hydroponic systems are not suited to swelling root tubers.

toxicity.note: Beetroot is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs

name: Zucchini
description: A prolific summer squash producing tender green fruits quickly throughout the season.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply every day at the base; avoid wetting the leaves to prevent mildew.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6–8 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with phosphorus- and potassium-rich fertilizer once fruiting starts.
careDetails.pruning: Remove old lower leaves for airflow; harvest fruit young and often.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew, squash vine borers, and poor fruit set without pollinators.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1800–2400 ppm (EC 3.5–4.8) — increase during fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 45–60 days from transplant; harvest fruits at 15–20 cm continuously.
hidroponik.tips: Zucchini grows large, so use a sturdy Dutch Bucket system with strong aeration. Hand-pollinate the flowers indoors since natural pollinators are absent.

toxicity.note: Zucchini is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs

name: Arugula
description: A fast-growing salad green with a peppery, nutty flavor, harvestable within weeks.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium evenly moist; water daily so leaves stay tender and mild.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; some shade keeps leaves from turning bitter.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light nitrogen-rich liquid feed every 2 weeks for fast leaf growth.
careDetails.pruning: Pick outer leaves first as a cut-and-come-again crop for repeat harvests.
careDetails.commonProblems: Flea beetles, aphids, and quick bolting (flowering) in hot weather.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 20–30 days after transplanting; harvest young for the mildest flavor.
hidroponik.tips: Arugula is one of the fastest hydroponic greens and great for beginners. Keep the solution cool and harvest young, since heat triggers bolting and bitterness.

toxicity.note: Arugula is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs

name: Lemon
description: A fragrant evergreen citrus tree producing tart, juicy yellow fruit; it grows well in a large pot on a sunny patio.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply every 2-3 days, letting the top 3 cm dry between waterings; avoid waterlogging.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6-8 hours of direct light per day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a citrus-specific fertilizer high in nitrogen and micronutrients once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune dead or crossing branches and thin the canopy each spring to improve airflow and light.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellowing leaves from nutrient deficiency, plus aphids, scale, and citrus leaf miner.

hidroponik.tips: Lemon is a woody tree with deep roots and a heavy fruiting canopy, so it needs soil anchorage and is not practical for home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Citrus oils and psoralens cause vomiting, diarrhea in pets

name: Pomegranate
description: A hardy, drought-tolerant shrub bearing leathery red fruit packed with jewel-like, juicy seeds (arils).

careDetails.watering: Water every 3-5 days once established; it tolerates dry spells but fruits best with steady moisture during fruiting.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day for the best flowering and fruit set.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced NPK fertilizer monthly, reducing nitrogen as fruit develops.
careDetails.pruning: Remove suckers and thin inner branches in late winter to shape the shrub and boost fruiting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit splitting from irregular watering, plus aphids and fruit borer.

hidroponik.tips: Pomegranate is a long-lived woody shrub with an extensive root system, so it is grown in soil or large pots rather than home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Bark, root, peel may cause vomiting; flesh mild

name: Dragon Fruit
description: A climbing cactus producing striking pink-skinned fruit with sweet, speckled flesh; it thrives in pots with a sturdy support.

careDetails.watering: Water sparingly, about once a week, letting the medium dry well; as a cactus it rots easily if kept wet.
careDetails.sunlight: Full to partial sun; very strong midday sun can scorch the stems.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a low-nitrogen, high-potassium fertilizer monthly to encourage flowering and fruit.
careDetails.pruning: Trim overcrowded and old stems after harvest to keep the plant manageable and productive.
careDetails.commonProblems: Stem rot from overwatering and poor drainage; sunburn on exposed stems.

hidroponik.tips: As a heavy climbing cactus that needs a dry root zone and a strong trellis, dragon fruit is far better suited to soil or large pots than to hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Dragon fruit flesh is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Pineapple
description: A tropical bromeliad with a rosette of spiky leaves that produces one sweet, golden fruit per plant; easy to grow from a cut-off crown.

careDetails.watering: Water every 4-5 days; it stores water in its leaves and dislikes soggy soil.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, 6 or more hours daily for sweet fruit.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed monthly with a balanced fertilizer; it can also absorb nutrients through its leaves.
careDetails.pruning: Remove dead lower leaves and offshoots (pups), which can be replanted.
careDetails.commonProblems: Heart rot from water pooling in the crown; mealybugs.

hidroponik.tips: Pineapple takes 18-24 months to fruit and prefers a dry, free-draining root zone, so soil or a large pot is more practical than a home hydroponic setup.

toxicity.note: Flesh safe; raw bromelain may irritate mouth, stomach

name: Raspberry
description: A thorny cane shrub bearing soft, sweet-tart red berries; it does best in cool climates or highland gardens.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist, watering every 1-2 days in warm weather, but avoid waterlogging.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to light shade; appreciates protection from intense midday heat.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced or fruit fertilizer every 2-3 weeks during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Cut spent fruiting canes to the ground after harvest and thin new canes to a few strong ones.
careDetails.commonProblems: Cane borers, gray mold (botrytis), and root rot in heavy, wet soil.

hidroponik.tips: Raspberry spreads by perennial canes and suckering roots that need room in soil, making it impractical for a tidy home hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Raspberry is non-toxic; large amounts may cause mild upset

name: Mango
description: A large tropical tree prized for its fragrant, sweet golden fruit; dwarf varieties can be grown in big pots.

careDetails.watering: Water every 3-5 days; reduce watering before flowering to encourage blooms, then resume during fruiting.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day for strong growth and good fruiting.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply manure plus an NPK fertilizer every 6 weeks, favoring potassium as fruit forms.
careDetails.pruning: Prune after harvest to shape the canopy, remove dead wood, and keep the tree at a manageable height.
careDetails.commonProblems: Anthracnose on flowers and fruit, fruit flies, and powdery mildew.

hidroponik.tips: Mango is a large, deep-rooted tree that needs soil anchorage for its heavy canopy and fruit, making home hydroponics unfeasible.

toxicity.note: Flesh safe; skin, pit cause choking, urushiol irritation

name: Chamomile
description: A daisy-like herb with apple-scented flowers, prized for calming herbal teas and soothing aromatherapy.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2 cm of medium is dry, roughly every 2-3 days; avoid waterlogging.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun for at least 6 hours; tolerates light afternoon shade.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a light dose of balanced liquid fertilizer once a month; over-feeding reduces flower fragrance.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back leggy stems and deadhead spent flowers regularly to encourage continuous blooming.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew and aphids in humid, poorly ventilated conditions.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700–1000 ppm (EC 1.4–2.0) — keep it moderate to preserve the floral aroma
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–75 days from sowing — harvest the open flower heads in the morning
hidroponik.tips: Sow seeds in rockwool and keep them shallow since chamomile seed needs light to germinate. As a soft annual herb it adapts well to NFT; harvest flowers regularly to keep production going.

toxicity.note: Contains anthemic acid and volatile oils — can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and contact dermatitis in cats and dogs

name: Jasmine
description: A woody climbing vine with intensely fragrant white flowers, a classic source of sweet, romantic aromatherapy oil.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days, keeping the medium moist but never soggy.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to bright partial shade; more sun means more flowers.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a high-phosphorus flowering fertilizer every month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune after flowering to control the vines and encourage bushier growth.
careDetails.commonProblems: Spider mites, mealybugs, and bud drop if it dries out during budding.

hidroponik.tips: Jasmine is a woody perennial vine with an extensive root system and needs a stable anchored medium to support its climbing growth, so it is not suited to home hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: True jasmine (Jasminum officinale) is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs

name: Scented Geranium
description: A bushy herb with rose-scented, deeply lobed leaves used to produce geranium essential oil for aromatherapy and perfumery.

careDetails.watering: Water every 3-4 days; let the top of the medium dry out as it dislikes constant wetness.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to bright light brings out the strongest leaf fragrance.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a balanced liquid fertilizer every month, lighter doses for stronger scent.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch growing tips regularly to keep the plant compact and harvest aromatic leaves.
careDetails.commonProblems: Stem and root rot from overwatering; whiteflies on the undersides of leaves.

hidroponik.media: Rockwool + coarse perlite for sharp drainage
hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700–1000 ppm (EC 1.4–2.0) — keep it lean to maximize the leaf oil
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days — harvest aromatic leaves and stem tips continuously
hidroponik.tips: Scented geranium can be grown hydroponically but with caution: it hates wet feet, so provide vigorous aeration and keep part of the root zone above the solution to prevent rot. Root a stem cutting in water first before transferring.

toxicity.note: Geraniol and linalool in Pelargonium — can cause vomiting, anorexia, and dermatitis in cats and dogs

name: Eucalyptus
description: A fast-growing aromatic tree whose menthol-fresh leaves yield the cineole-rich oil used to clear airways in aromatherapy.

careDetails.watering: Water every 3-4 days when young; established plants tolerate short dry spells.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day for vigorous, aromatic growth.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a slow-release NPK fertilizer every 2 months; avoid excess phosphorus.
careDetails.pruning: Prune or coppice regularly to keep it shrub-sized and harvest the young aromatic foliage.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot in heavy, waterlogged soil and leaf spot in overly humid conditions.

hidroponik.tips: Eucalyptus is a woody, fast-growing tree with a deep, aggressive root system and large eventual size, making it unsuitable for home hydroponic setups.

toxicity.note: Eucalyptol (cineole) and essential oils — can cause drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, and depression in cats and dogs

name: Echinacea
description: The purple coneflower, a hardy medicinal herb widely used to support the immune system and shorten colds.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days while establishing; mature plants are quite drought-tolerant.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun for the best flowering and root development.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a light dose of balanced fertilizer once a month; it thrives in lean conditions.
careDetails.pruning: Deadhead spent flowers to prolong blooming, or leave them to harvest seed.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew, aster yellows, and crown rot in poorly drained soil.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Harvest leaves and flowers within 60–90 days; roots take a full season
hidroponik.tips: Echinacea germinates slowly, so cold-stratify seed before sowing in rockwool. It grows well in DWC for leaf and flower harvest, though the medicinal roots develop best given a long, undisturbed cycle.

toxicity.note: Echinacea is generally non-toxic to cats and dogs, though large amounts may cause mild stomach upset

name: Ashwagandha
description: An Ayurvedic adaptogen shrub whose roots are valued for reducing stress and boosting stamina.

careDetails.watering: Water every 4-5 days; let the medium dry between waterings as it dislikes wet roots.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun in a warm, dry spot is ideal.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a light compost or balanced fertilizer every 2 months; it prefers poor soil.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch tips to encourage bushiness; minimal pruning is needed overall.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering and leaf spot during humid rainy spells.

hidroponik.media: Rockwool + coarse perlite for strong drainage
hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700–1000 ppm (EC 1.4–2.0) — keep it lean as this plant likes dry, poor conditions
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Leaves in 60–90 days; the medicinal roots need 150–180 days to mature
hidroponik.tips: Ashwagandha can be grown hydroponically but is naturally a dryland shrub, so provide strong aeration, lean nutrients, and avoid constant saturation. The valuable roots develop best in a deep DWC bucket over a long cycle.

toxicity.note: Limited pet safety data; as a potent adaptogen with steroidal lactones it should be given to cats and dogs only with caution

name: Money Tree
description: A popular indoor tree with a braided trunk and glossy palmate leaves, said to bring good fortune and very forgiving to grow.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply once a week, letting the top 3-5 cm of soil dry out between waterings; it tolerates occasional missed watering.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; can adapt to medium light but avoid harsh direct sun that scorches the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season, none in winter.
careDetails.pruning: Trim leggy stems and remove yellowing leaves every few months to keep a compact shape.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellowing or dropping leaves from overwatering and root rot are the most common issues.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — steady growth visible within 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: Money Tree transitions well to semi-hydroponics; rinse all soil off the roots first and keep only the lower third in solution to prevent stem rot.

toxicity.note: Pachira aquatica is non-toxic to cats and dogs (ASPCA)

name: Bromeliad
description: A tropical bromeliad prized for its long-lasting, vivid red or orange flower bract rising from a rosette of strappy green leaves.

careDetails.watering: Keep the central cup filled with clean water and water the medium lightly when its surface dries, roughly every 5 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; too little light dulls the bract color, too much scorches the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Use a diluted liquid fertilizer every two months, applied to the medium rather than the central cup.
careDetails.pruning: Cut the spent flower bract once it fades and let the offset pups grow on to replace the dying mother plant.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rot in the central cup from stagnant dirty water, and brown leaf tips from dry air.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) — mimics its airy epiphytic root zone
hidroponik.nutrisi: Very diluted AB Mix 400-600 ppm (EC 0.8-1.2) — a light feeder
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — flowers after 1-3 years, then pups continue the cycle
hidroponik.tips: As an epiphyte, Guzmania does well in LECA; keep the central cup topped with clean water and never fully submerge the base of the rosette.

toxicity.note: Guzmania bromeliads are non-toxic to cats and dogs (ASPCA)

name: Lucky Bamboo
description: Not a true bamboo but a Dracaena, famous for thriving in a vase of water with green stalks often trained into spirals.

careDetails.watering: If grown in soil, keep it lightly moist; if grown in water, change the water weekly and keep the roots submerged.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; direct sun yellows the stalks while deep shade weakens growth.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed very lightly with diluted liquid fertilizer every two months; water-grown plants need only a tiny dose.
careDetails.pruning: Trim off yellow leaves and cut leggy stalks above a node to encourage new shoots.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellowing from chlorinated tap water or too much fertilizer; algae and rot in dirty vase water.

hidroponik.media: Clean water with decorative pebbles to anchor the stalks
hidroponik.nutrisi: Very diluted AB Mix 300-500 ppm (EC 0.6-1.0), or plain dechlorinated water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new shoots appear within 1-2 months
hidroponik.tips: Lucky Bamboo naturally grows in water and is the easiest plant for hydro; use dechlorinated or rainwater, change it weekly, and keep the roots covered while the stalks stay above the waterline.

toxicity.note: Dracaena sanderiana is toxic to cats and dogs — saponins cause vomiting, drooling, weakness (ASPCA)

name: Rex Begonia
description: A foliage begonia grown for its dramatic spiral leaves splashed with silver, purple, green and pink markings.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2 cm of medium is dry, every 2-3 days; keep it evenly moist but never soggy.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light brings out the leaf colors; direct sun fades and burns the foliage.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply diluted balanced liquid fertilizer monthly through the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Remove old or damaged leaves at the base and pinch back to keep the plant full.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew in stagnant humid air and root rot if the medium stays waterlogged.

hidroponik.tips: Rex Begonia has delicate rhizomes and fleshy stems that rot easily when submerged, so it is not suited to hydroponic systems and grows best in a light, airy soil mix.

toxicity.note: Begonia is toxic to cats and dogs — soluble calcium oxalates, most concentrated in the tubers (ASPCA)

name: Calathea Orbifolia
description: A striking prayer plant with large round leaves banded in silvery-green stripes that fold up at night.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium lightly and evenly moist, watering every 2-3 days; it is sensitive to drying out and to mineral-heavy water.
careDetails.sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light; direct sun bleaches and curls the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with diluted liquid fertilizer monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Snip off browning or crispy leaves at the base; otherwise it needs little pruning.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown crispy leaf edges from low humidity or tap-water minerals, and curling from underwatering.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new leaves unfurl within 1-2 months once acclimated
hidroponik.tips: Calathea can move to LECA but hates change; transition slowly, use only filtered or rainwater to avoid brown tips, and keep humidity high during acclimation.

toxicity.note: Calathea / Goeppertia is non-toxic to cats and dogs (ASPCA)

name: Philodendron Brasil
description: A fast-growing trailing heartleaf philodendron with green leaves splashed by a lime-yellow variegated stripe.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2-3 cm of medium is dry, roughly every 3-4 days; tolerant of occasional neglect.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light keeps the variegation strong; too little light reverts leaves to plain green.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply liquid NPK fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back the vine tips to encourage bushier growth and trim any all-green or leggy stems.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellowing leaves from overwatering and loss of variegation in low light.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — active vine growth visible within 1-2 months
hidroponik.tips: Philodendron Brasil roots readily in water; place stem cuttings in clean water for about 2 weeks until roots form, then move them into the nutrient solution.

toxicity.note: Philodendron is toxic to cats and dogs — insoluble calcium oxalates cause oral irritation, drooling, vomiting (ASPCA)

name: Arrowhead Plant
description: A fast-growing trailing houseplant prized for its arrow-shaped leaves that shift color and form as the plant matures.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2-3 cm of the medium is dry, roughly every 3-4 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates moderate shade but variegation fades in deep shade.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back leggy vines to keep the plant bushy and remove yellowing leaves.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellow leaves from overwatering and spider mites in dry air.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — vigorous vining growth visible within 1-2 months
hidroponik.tips: Roots easily in plain water, making it one of the easiest plants for semi-hydroponics. Change the solution every 2 weeks and keep an air gap above it.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, drooling, vomiting if chewed

name: Swiss Cheese Vine
description: A trailing tropical vine famous for its delicate leaves dotted with natural holes, perfect for hanging baskets.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2-3 cm of the medium dries out, about every 3-4 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; direct sun scorches the thin leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Liquid foliage fertilizer once a month while actively growing.
careDetails.pruning: Trim long vines to encourage branching and propagate cuttings easily.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaves without holes indicate too little light; root rot from soggy medium.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — lush new vines visible within 1-2 months
hidroponik.tips: Cuttings with a node root readily in water. Give the vines a moss pole or trellis for larger, more fenestrated leaves.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — mouth/throat irritation, drooling if chewed

name: Cast Iron Plant
description: An almost indestructible foliage plant with broad dark-green leaves that thrives in deep shade and neglect.

careDetails.watering: Water sparingly, letting the medium dry between waterings, about once a week.
careDetails.sunlight: Low to moderate indirect light; one of the few plants that tolerates deep shade.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light liquid fertilizer every 2 months during spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Simply remove old or damaged leaves at the base.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from direct sun or over-fertilizing; very pest-resistant.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Very dilute AB Mix 400-700 ppm (EC 0.8-1.4) — a light feeder
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — slow steady growth, new leaves over 3-5 months
hidroponik.tips: Adapts to semi-hydroponics but grows slowly, so rinse the system and refresh nutrients only every 3-4 weeks to avoid salt buildup.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA; safe houseplant

name: Coffee Plant
description: The arabica coffee shrub grown indoors for its glossy dark leaves, fragrant white flowers, and red cherries.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium consistently moist but never waterlogged, about every 3 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; protect from harsh direct midday sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Acidic-formula liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune in early spring to maintain a compact, bushy shape.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf browning from dry air or fluoride in tap water; mealybugs and scale.

hidroponik.media: Coco coir mixed with perlite, or hydroton
hidroponik.nutrisi: Standard AB Mix 1000-1400 ppm (EC 2.0-2.8) with extra micronutrients
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Cherries appear after 3-4 years; grown mainly as foliage indoors
hidroponik.tips: A long-term woody plant that needs a large root volume and stable warmth (20-28°C). Keep the root zone slightly acidic and well oxygenated.

toxicity.note: Caffeine in leaves and cherries — vomiting, tremors, rapid heartbeat

name: Purple Heart
description: A striking trailing plant with deep purple, lance-shaped leaves and small pink flowers, loved for its bold color.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the medium dries, about every 4 days; tolerates short drought.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light, including some direct sun, intensifies the purple color.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month in spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch tips frequently to keep it dense; cuttings root almost instantly.
careDetails.commonProblems: Green, leggy growth in low light; sap can irritate skin.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — fast trailing growth visible within 3-6 weeks
hidroponik.tips: One of the easiest plants to root in water; just drop a cutting in. Give it bright light or the leaves revert to green instead of purple.

toxicity.note: Sap may cause skin and mild GI irritation in pets

name: Watermelon Peperomia
description: A compact tabletop plant with rounded leaves striped silver and green like a watermelon rind.

careDetails.watering: Let the medium dry out partway before watering, roughly every 5-7 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light keeps the leaf pattern crisp.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted liquid fertilizer every 6 weeks during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Remove faded leaves; it stays naturally compact with little pruning.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering its succulent stems; leggy in low light.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Dilute AB Mix 500-800 ppm (EC 1.0-1.6) — sensitive to over-feeding
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — compact growth, new leaves over 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: Its fleshy stems rot easily, so keep only the bottom of the roots in solution and use a well-aerated wick or semi-hydro setup.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA; pet-safe

name: Zebra Haworthia
description: A small, slow-growing succulent with dark green leaves striped by white tubercles, perfect for sunny windowsills.

careDetails.watering: Water every 10-14 days, only after the medium is completely dry.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates a few hours of gentle morning sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted cactus fertilizer once every 2 months during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Minimal; remove dried outer leaves and separate offsets when crowded.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering and leaf shriveling under harsh midday sun.

hidroponik.tips: As a drought-adapted succulent, Zebra Haworthia stores water in its leaves and rots quickly when roots stay wet, so hydroponics is not recommended.

toxicity.note: Haworthia is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Burro's Tail
description: A trailing succulent with plump, blue-green leaves forming long braided tails, ideal for hanging baskets.

careDetails.watering: Water every 10-14 days, letting the medium dry out fully between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light with some direct morning sun for the fullest leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light succulent fertilizer once every 2 months in spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Trim overly long stems; the detached beads root easily as new plants.
careDetails.commonProblems: Beads drop off when handled, and stems rot if the soil stays soggy.

hidroponik.tips: Burro's Tail is a succulent that hoards water in its fleshy leaves and is highly prone to rot, making constantly wet hydroponic roots unsuitable.

toxicity.note: Burro's Tail is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Ti Plant
description: A tropical shrub prized for its bold, strappy leaves in vivid shades of pink, red, and green.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium evenly moist; water every 2-3 days, never letting it dry out.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; more light deepens the leaf colors.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Cut back leggy canes to encourage bushier new shoots from the base.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from fluoride in tap water or dry air.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — vivid new growth visible within 2–3 months
hidroponik.tips: Root a healthy stem cutting in plain water for 2–3 weeks before moving to nutrient solution. Use non-fluoridated water to avoid leaf-tip burn.

toxicity.note: Saponins — vomiting, drooling, and dilated pupils in cats.

name: Maidenhair Fern
description: A delicate fern with airy, fan-shaped fronds on fine black stems that loves humid, shaded spots.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium constantly moist; water every 1-2 days and never let it dry.
careDetails.sunlight: Low to moderate indirect light; avoid all direct sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Very diluted liquid fertilizer once a month during active growth.
careDetails.pruning: Snip off browned or crispy fronds at the base to encourage fresh growth.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fronds crisp and brown quickly if humidity drops or the soil dries out.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) with a constant shallow water reservoir
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — lush new fronds appear within 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: This fern thrives in the constant moisture of semi-hydro as long as humidity stays high. Use dechlorinated water and keep roots wicking, not fully drowned.

toxicity.note: Maidenhair Fern is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Staghorn Fern
description: An epiphytic fern with dramatic antler-shaped fronds, usually mounted on a board or wood.

careDetails.watering: Soak the root ball weekly, then let it drain; mist between soakings.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid harsh direct midday sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted balanced fertilizer once every 2 months in the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Leave the brown shield fronds intact; only remove fully dead antler fronds.
careDetails.commonProblems: Black, mushy fronds from overwatering or poor air circulation.

hidroponik.tips: Staghorn Fern is an epiphyte that absorbs water and air through mounted root balls and needs to dry between soakings, so submerged hydroponic roots cause rot.

toxicity.note: Staghorn Fern is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Aluminum Plant
description: A compact foliage plant with green leaves splashed by shimmering silver, aluminum-like patches.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the medium feels dry, roughly every 3-4 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light keeps the silver markings vivid.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back the growing tips regularly to keep the plant bushy and compact.
careDetails.commonProblems: Legginess in low light and leaf curl if the soil dries out too long.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) or roots directly in water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — bushy new growth visible within 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: Stem cuttings root readily in plain water within 1–2 weeks, making this an easy beginner hydroponic plant. Pinch tips often to keep it full.

toxicity.note: Aluminum Plant is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Asparagus Fern
description: A feathery, fern-like foliage plant with soft, lacy green fronds that bring an airy, delicate texture indoors.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil lightly moist; water when the top 2 cm feels dry, about every 3 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright, indirect light; avoid harsh direct sun that scorches the fronds.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim yellowed or browned fronds at the base to keep the plant tidy and full.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fronds turn yellow and drop in dry air or under inconsistent watering; spider mites can appear.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — lush frond growth visible within 2–3 months
hidroponik.tips: Keep humidity high and let the upper roots breathe; rinse media occasionally to prevent salt buildup.

toxicity.note: Berries toxic; sap irritates skin and stomach

name: Stromanthe Triostar
description: A striking prayer plant with variegated leaves splashed in cream, green, and pink, with deep maroon undersides.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist but not soggy; water when the top layer begins to dry, about every 3 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright, indirect light; direct sun fades the variegation.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a diluted balanced fertilizer once a month while growing.
careDetails.pruning: Remove dead or curled leaves at the base to encourage fresh growth.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf edges brown from low humidity or hard tap water; leaves curl when too dry.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — full colorful foliage visible within 3–4 months
hidroponik.tips: Use rainwater or filtered water to avoid leaf burn, and keep humidity high for vivid variegation.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs

name: Lipstick Plant
description: A trailing houseplant with glossy leaves and tubular red flowers that emerge from dark buds like a lipstick tube.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2–3 cm of soil is dry, about every 4 days; reduce in winter.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright, indirect light encourages abundant blooming.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a bloom-boosting liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back leggy stems after flowering to keep the plant bushy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Failure to bloom from too little light; leaf drop in cold drafts or overwatering.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — first blooms can appear within 3–4 months
hidroponik.tips: Provide bright light and high humidity to trigger flowering; let the upper roots stay aerated.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs

name: Kentia Palm
description: An elegant, slow-growing indoor palm with gracefully arching dark-green fronds, prized for its tolerance of low light.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top few centimeters of soil dry out, about every 5 days; avoid waterlogging.
careDetails.sunlight: Tolerates low to bright indirect light; keep away from harsh direct sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a balanced palm fertilizer every 2 months during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Only remove fully browned or dead fronds at the base.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from dry air or fluoride in tap water; spider mites in dry conditions.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — slow, steady growth over 4–6 months
hidroponik.tips: Transition young plants gently and keep the solution well-aerated; this palm grows slowly, so change water every 3–4 weeks.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs

name: Philodendron Birkin
description: A compact philodendron with dark green leaves striped in fine creamy-white pinstripes, easy and rewarding to grow.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2–3 cm of soil is dry, about every 4 days; let excess drain freely.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright, indirect light keeps the variegated pinstripes crisp.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim damaged leaves at the base; wipe foliage to keep it clean.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellow leaves from overwatering; loss of variegation in low light.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — steady leaf growth visible within 2–3 months
hidroponik.tips: Philodendrons root readily in water; leave an air gap above the solution and change it every 2 weeks.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, drooling, vomiting

name: Anthurium Clarinervium
description: A collector's foliage plant with large, heart-shaped velvety dark-green leaves dramatically veined in silvery white.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the chunky mix dries, about every 4 days; never let it stay soggy.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright, indirect light; direct sun scorches the velvety leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a diluted balanced fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Remove old or damaged leaves at the base; this plant needs little pruning.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot in dense soil; brown leaf edges and stalled growth in low humidity.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new velvety leaves emerge every 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: Demands very high humidity and warm, aerated roots; keep the solution weak and well-oxygenated to avoid rot.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, drooling, vomiting

name: String of Bananas
description: A trailing succulent with plump, banana-shaped leaves that cascade beautifully from hanging pots.

careDetails.watering: Water only when the soil is fully dry, roughly every 10 days; it stores water in its leaves.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light to a little morning sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted succulent fertilizer once every 2 months during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim long or leggy strands to keep the plant full and bushy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Shriveled leaves from underwatering; mushy stems from overwatering.

hidroponik.tips: As a drought-tolerant succulent, String of Bananas rots quickly with constantly wet roots and is not suited to hydroponic systems; use a fast-draining cactus mix instead.

toxicity.note: Toxic to cats and dogs; causes vomiting, drooling, and lethargy.

name: Lace Aloe
description: A compact rosette succulent with dark green leaves edged in soft white teeth and lacy bristles.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply when the soil is dry, about every 10 days; reduce in winter.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light with a few hours of direct sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light succulent fertilizer once every 2 months in spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Remove dried outer leaves and separate offsets to propagate.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from soggy soil; etiolation if light is too low.

hidroponik.tips: Lace Aloe is a desert succulent whose roots rot in permanently wet conditions, so it is not suited to hydroponics; grow it in a gritty, fast-draining mix.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs; safe around pets.

name: Ctenanthe
description: A prayer-plant relative prized for its silvery leaves boldly striped in dark green, called the fishbone plant.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium lightly moist, watering about every 4 days; avoid letting it dry out fully.
careDetails.sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light; no direct sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month while actively growing.
careDetails.pruning: Trim browned or damaged leaves at the base to keep the clump tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Crispy leaf edges from dry air or low humidity; fading color in deep shade.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) in a self-watering pot keeps humidity steady at the roots
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — lush new foliage appears within 2–3 months
hidroponik.tips: Ctenanthe loves humidity, making semi-hydro with LECA a great fit. Keep the reservoir below the root crown and flush the LECA monthly to prevent salt buildup.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs; safe around pets.

name: Goldfish Plant
description: A trailing houseplant with glossy leaves and puffy orange flowers shaped like leaping goldfish.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2 cm of medium is dry, about every 5 days; it likes slight moisture.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light to encourage flowering.
careDetails.fertilizer: Liquid bloom fertilizer every 2 weeks during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back trailing stems regularly to stay bushy and bloom more.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf drop from cold drafts; few flowers if light is too low.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) provides airy, evenly moist roots
hidroponik.nutrisi: Diluted AB Mix 700–1000 ppm (EC 1.4–2.0) with extra bloom support
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — flowering returns within 3–4 months once settled
hidroponik.tips: Goldfish Plant adapts well to LECA semi-hydro. Keep humidity up and the reservoir shallow so the roots stay airy, which keeps blooms coming.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs; safe around pets.

name: Majesty Palm
description: A graceful indoor palm with arching, feathery fronds that brings a lush tropical feel to any room.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium consistently moist, watering about every 4 days; it dislikes drying out.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light with some filtered sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced palm fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Cut off fully browned fronds at the base; leave green ones intact.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from dry air or fluoride in water; spider mites in low humidity.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) suits this moisture-loving palm well
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new fronds emerge within 2–3 months
hidroponik.tips: Majesty Palm thrives in LECA semi-hydro because it loves steady moisture. Use a generous reservoir and flush monthly to avoid salt buildup that browns the tips.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs; safe around pets.

name: Sweetheart Hoya
description: A succulent vine famous for its thick, heart-shaped leaves, often sold as a single rooted heart.

careDetails.watering: Water sparingly once the soil is dry, about every 12 days; the leaves store water.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light encourages growth and eventual blooms.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted fertilizer once every 2 months during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Minimal; never cut the flowering spurs as blooms form on them again.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rot from overwatering; a single leaf may never vine without a node.

hidroponik.tips: As a succulent vine, Sweetheart Hoya stores water in its leaves and rots with constantly wet roots, so it is not suited to hydroponics; use a chunky, fast-draining mix.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs; safe around pets.

name: Orange
description: A popular citrus tree producing sweet, juicy fruit rich in vitamin C, with fragrant white blossoms.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply every 2-3 days; let the topsoil dry slightly between waterings to avoid root rot.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun for at least 6-8 hours a day for the best fruiting.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a nitrogen-rich citrus fertilizer monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune dead or crossing branches after harvest to keep the canopy open and airy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Citrus leaf miner, scale insects, and yellowing leaves from poor drainage or zinc deficiency.

hidroponik.tips: Orange is a woody fruit tree with a deep taproot and a large canopy, so it is not suitable for home hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Citrus oils and psoralens cause vomiting, drooling, and skin irritation in pets

name: Lychee
description: A subtropical tree bearing sweet, fragrant fruit with rough red skin and translucent juicy flesh.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist but never waterlogged; water every 2-3 days in dry weather.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, sheltered from strong wind that can damage flowers and young fruit.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a balanced fertilizer monthly, switching to high potassium before flowering.
careDetails.pruning: Prune lightly after harvest to shape the canopy and remove weak inner branches.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit cracking, irregular flowering, and fruit borer attacks during humid seasons.

hidroponik.tips: Lychee is a large, long-lived woody tree that needs deep soil to anchor its roots, making hydroponics impractical.

toxicity.note: Flesh is safe, but seeds are toxic and unripe fruit can cause hypoglycemia

name: Longan
description: A tropical evergreen tree producing clusters of small round fruit with sweet, translucent flesh and a dark seed.

careDetails.watering: Water regularly every 2-3 days, increasing during flowering and fruit set.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun in a warm, frost-free location for reliable fruiting.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply organic compost and balanced NPK every month during active growth.
careDetails.pruning: Prune after harvest to control height and encourage new fruiting wood.
careDetails.commonProblems: Erratic flowering, fruit drop, and witches' broom disease spread by mites.

hidroponik.tips: Longan grows into a tall woody tree with extensive roots, so it cannot be grown in a home hydroponic setup.

toxicity.note: Flesh is safe but the large seed poses a toxicity and choking risk to pets

name: Rambutan
description: A tropical tree famous for its hairy red fruit with sweet, juicy white flesh wrapped around a single seed.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days to keep soil moist; rambutan dislikes prolonged drought.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun with high humidity gives the sweetest, most abundant fruit.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with organic manure and balanced NPK monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Remove dead and overcrowded branches after harvest to improve airflow and light.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit borers, mealybugs, and fruit splitting caused by uneven watering.

hidroponik.tips: Rambutan is a large evergreen fruit tree that needs deep soil for its root system, so hydroponics is not feasible.

toxicity.note: Flesh is non-toxic and safe; remove the seed before feeding pets

name: Soursop
description: A small tropical tree bearing large green spiny fruit with creamy, tangy-sweet white pulp.

careDetails.watering: Keep soil evenly moist; water every 2-3 days and avoid letting it dry out fully.
careDetails.sunlight: Full to partial sun in a warm, sheltered, frost-free spot.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced fertilizer with compost every month for steady growth.
careDetails.pruning: Prune to keep the tree compact and remove weak or dead branches yearly.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, anthracnose, and poor fruit set without hand pollination.

hidroponik.tips: Soursop is a woody fruit tree that develops a deep root system, so it is not suited to home hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Seeds and bark contain annonacin, a neurotoxin; keep seeds away from pets

name: Sugar Apple
description: A small tree producing knobby green fruit with sweet, custard-like white flesh and many black seeds.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; it tolerates short dry spells but fruits best with steady moisture.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day for sweet, well-formed fruit.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed monthly with balanced NPK and compost during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune annually to keep the tree low and open for easier harvesting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, fruit borers, and poor fruit set that often needs hand pollination.

hidroponik.tips: Sugar apple is a woody fruit tree that requires deep soil for its roots, making it unsuitable for hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Seeds and bark are toxic, containing annonacin; flesh is safe in moderation

name: Jackfruit
description: The world's largest tree-borne fruit, with sweet golden flesh and a strong aroma; a popular shade tree in Indonesian yards.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days while young; mature trees are fairly drought tolerant but fruit better with steady moisture.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day long.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply manure plus balanced NPK once a month, switching to high-potassium fertilizer before fruiting.
careDetails.pruning: Prune to keep the canopy open and remove dead or crossing branches after harvest.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit borer and stem rot in poorly drained soil.

hidroponik.tips: Jackfruit is a massive tree that needs deep soil to anchor its strong roots and support heavy fruit, so it is not suitable for home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Jackfruit flesh is non-toxic to dogs and cats; remove the large seeds and feed in moderation.

name: Durian
description: The "king of fruits" — a spiky tropical fruit with rich, custard-like flesh and a famously pungent aroma.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days and keep soil consistently moist; durian dislikes drought but also rots in waterlogged ground.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, in a warm, humid, sheltered location.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply organic matter plus NPK once a month, increasing potassium and phosphorus as flowering approaches.
careDetails.pruning: Shape the canopy and remove dead or weak branches; thin excess fruit so the tree is not overburdened.
careDetails.commonProblems: Phytophthora root and stem rot, plus fruit borer attacking developing fruit.

hidroponik.tips: Durian is a tall tree with deep roots and very heavy fruit; it requires deep soil and cannot be grown in a home hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Durian flesh is non-toxic but very high in fat and sugar; offer only a tiny amount and never the seeds.

name: Mangosteen
description: The "queen of fruits" — a deep-purple tropical fruit with snow-white, sweet-tangy segments inside a thick rind.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days and never let the soil dry out; mangosteen needs high, steady humidity.
careDetails.sunlight: Partial shade when young, gradually moving to full sun as it matures.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply compost plus balanced NPK once a month; growth is slow so feed steadily.
careDetails.pruning: Minimal pruning — only remove dead or damaged branches to keep the form tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Very slow growth and leaf scorch if exposed to harsh sun or dry air too early.

hidroponik.tips: Mangosteen is a slow-growing tree with a deep taproot that demands deep, rich soil, so it is unsuitable for home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Mangosteen flesh is non-toxic to dogs and cats; remove the rind and seeds and feed only the soft segments in moderation.

name: Salak (Snake Fruit)
description: A short, thorny palm bearing clusters of fruit with reddish-brown scaly skin and crisp, sweet-astringent flesh.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; salak likes moist soil and high humidity but not standing water.
careDetails.sunlight: Partial shade to filtered light; it naturally grows under a taller canopy.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply manure plus NPK once a month for steady leaf and fruit production.
careDetails.pruning: Carefully remove old, dried, thorny fronds at the base; wear thick gloves.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs hiding among the fruit clusters and rot in waterlogged soil.

hidroponik.tips: Salak is a clumping palm with a dense, spreading root system that needs deep soil, so it cannot be grown hydroponically at home.

toxicity.note: Salak flesh is non-toxic to dogs and cats; peel the scaly skin and remove the seed before feeding small amounts.

name: Sapodilla (Sawo)
description: A hardy tropical tree with brown grainy-skinned fruit whose soft flesh tastes of malty brown sugar.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; once established it tolerates drought well, so reduce watering in the rainy season.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply compost plus NPK once a month for healthy growth and fruiting.
careDetails.pruning: Lightly prune to shape the canopy and remove dead wood after harvest.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies attacking ripening fruit and scale insects on the leaves.

hidroponik.tips: Sapodilla is a long-lived tree with a deep, extensive root system that requires deep soil, making it unsuitable for home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Ripe sapodilla flesh is generally safe, but unripe fruit and the bark contain a milky latex (saponin/tannins) and the seeds have a small hook that can cause GI upset or obstruction; feed only ripe, seedless flesh in moderation.

name: Persimmon
description: A deciduous tree bearing glossy orange fruit with sweet, honeyed flesh, best grown in cooler highland areas.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days, keeping the soil evenly moist especially while fruit is developing.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun; it benefits from a cool winter or highland climate.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced NPK plus compost once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune in the dormant season to open the canopy and remove crossing branches.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit drop from inconsistent watering and astringency if fruit is picked too early.

hidroponik.tips: Persimmon is a deciduous tree with a deep taproot that needs deep soil and a seasonal climate, so it is not suited to home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Persimmon flesh is non-toxic, but the seeds and pits can cause intestinal obstruction and inflammation (enteritis) in dogs; always remove all seeds and feed only small pieces of flesh.

name: Kiwi
description: A vigorous woody climbing vine producing fuzzy brown fruit with tangy, bright-green flesh dotted with tiny black seeds.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; the vine needs plenty of moisture but well-drained soil to avoid root rot.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun on a sturdy trellis or pergola for support.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced NPK plus compost once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune annually in the dormant season to control the vine and encourage fruiting wood.
careDetails.commonProblems: Needs both male and female vines to fruit; sensitive to strong wind and waterlogged roots.

hidroponik.tips: Kiwi is a large, heavy woody vine with extensive roots that needs deep soil and a strong trellis, so it is not practical for home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Kiwi flesh is non-toxic to dogs and cats; peel the skin and feed small pieces in moderation as it is high in sugar.

name: Apple
description: A classic deciduous orchard tree bearing crisp, sweet-tart fruit; grows best in cool highland regions like Malang.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days, keeping the soil evenly moist, especially while fruit is forming.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun; it needs a cool climate with a distinct cool period to fruit well.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced NPK plus compost once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune annually in the dormant season to shape the tree and encourage fruiting spurs.
careDetails.commonProblems: Apple scab, codling moth, and poor fruiting in climates that are too warm.

hidroponik.tips: Apple is a woody orchard tree with a deep root system that needs deep soil and a cool, seasonal climate, so it is unsuitable for home hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Apple flesh is non-toxic and safe for pets, but the seeds, leaves, and stems contain cyanogenic compounds (cyanide); always remove the core and seeds and feed only sliced flesh.

name: Cauliflower
description: A cool-season brassica grown for its dense white curd (head), rich in vitamin C and fiber.

careDetails.watering: Water consistently every day; the curd cracks if the soil dries out and gets soaked again.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, but prefers cooler temperatures (15-20°C) for good head formation.
careDetails.fertilizer: High-nitrogen fertilizer early, then balanced NPK once the head begins to form.
careDetails.pruning: Tie or fold the outer leaves over the curd (blanching) to keep it white and tender.
careDetails.commonProblems: Loose or 'ricey' curds in heat; cabbage worms and clubroot.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 70–90 days from transplanting — harvest while the curd is still tight
hidroponik.tips: Cauliflower needs cool, stable solution temperatures (under 24°C) to form a solid head. Keep the reservoir shaded and provide steady nutrition so growth never stalls.

toxicity.note: Brassica vegetable — safe in tiny amounts but causes gas, bloating and may affect the thyroid in excess.

name: Cabbage
description: A hardy leafy brassica that forms a tight round head, popular for stir-fries, salads and pickles.

careDetails.watering: Water every day to keep the soil evenly moist; uneven watering splits the head.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun; tolerates light shade and prefers cool weather.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply nitrogen-rich fertilizer every 2 weeks during leaf growth.
careDetails.pruning: Remove the lower yellowing leaves; harvest the whole head at the base when firm.
careDetails.commonProblems: Cabbage worms, aphids and head splitting after heavy rain.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days from transplanting depending on variety
hidroponik.tips: Cabbage heads up well in hydroponics if given wide spacing and steady high nutrition. Keep solution temperatures cool to prevent loose, leafy heads in tropical climates.

toxicity.note: Brassica vegetable — small amounts are fine but excess causes gas, bloating and can affect the thyroid.

name: Celery
description: A crisp, aromatic stalk vegetable used in soups and salads; both the stalks and leaves are edible.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil constantly moist; celery is thirsty and turns stringy if it dries out.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; prefers cool, mild conditions.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a nitrogen-rich liquid fertilizer every 2 weeks.
careDetails.pruning: Harvest the outer stalks first so the inner ones keep growing.
careDetails.commonProblems: Stringy, bitter stalks from drought; leaf blight and aphids.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 80–120 days from sowing — leaves can be picked earlier and continuously
hidroponik.tips: Celery thrives in constantly wet hydroponic roots, which actually suits its water-loving nature. It is slow from seed, so start in rockwool and be patient before transplanting into the NFT channel.

toxicity.note: Celery is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs in moderation.

name: Swiss Chard
description: A colorful leafy green with bright stalks, very productive and harvested leaf-by-leaf for months.

careDetails.watering: Water daily to keep the soil evenly moist for tender leaves.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; tolerates heat better than spinach.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a nitrogen-rich fertilizer every 2 weeks for continuous leaf production.
careDetails.pruning: Pick the outer leaves regularly and leave the center to keep regrowing.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf miners and downy mildew in humid, crowded plantings.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 30–45 days for first leaves, then continuous cut-and-come-again
hidroponik.tips: Swiss chard is one of the most rewarding leafy greens in hydroponics — harvest outer leaves and it keeps producing for months. It tolerates warm solution better than spinach, making it great for tropical setups.

toxicity.note: Contains oxalates — small amounts are fine but excess may cause stomach upset and affect calcium absorption.

name: Okra
description: A warm-season plant grown for its tender, ridged green pods, thriving in tropical heat.

careDetails.watering: Water every day during pod production; it is fairly drought-tolerant once established.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun and warm temperatures; loves heat.
careDetails.fertilizer: Use a balanced NPK fertilizer; avoid too much nitrogen which delays pods.
careDetails.pruning: Harvest pods young (5-8 cm) every 1-2 days; older pods turn woody and stop new ones.
