careDetails.commonProblems: Tough, fibrous pods if harvested late; aphids and root-knot nematodes.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 50–65 days from sowing — harvest pods continuously every 1–2 days
hidroponik.tips: Okra loves warmth and grows tall, so give it strong support and a deep, well-aerated reservoir. Keep harvesting young pods daily to keep the plant productive for months.

toxicity.note: Okra is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Garden Pea
description: A cool-season climbing legume grown for its sweet green pods and peas, fixing its own nitrogen.

careDetails.watering: Water daily to keep the soil moist, especially during flowering and pod set.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun; grows best in cool weather and bolts in heat.
careDetails.fertilizer: Low-nitrogen, higher-potassium fertilizer; peas fix their own nitrogen.
careDetails.pruning: Provide a trellis for the tendrils and harvest pods regularly to extend production.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew in warm, humid weather; aphids and pea weevils.

hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix 980–1260 ppm (EC 1.4–1.8) — keep nitrogen modest
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 55–70 days from sowing — pick pods while sweet and tender
hidroponik.tips: Peas do well in hydroponics with a trellis and a slightly lower-nitrogen solution since they prefer cool roots. Keep solution temperatures down and harvest often for a steady, sweet supply.

toxicity.note: Garden peas are non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Sweet Potato
description: A sprawling root crop grown from vine cuttings; its starchy tubers and edible leaves make it one of the most rewarding home garden staples.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; keep the soil moist but never waterlogged, and reduce watering as harvest nears.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a potassium-rich fertilizer; avoid excess nitrogen which grows leaves at the expense of tubers.
careDetails.pruning: Trim runaway vines occasionally so energy goes into the tubers instead of foliage.
careDetails.commonProblems: Tubers may be tunneled by sweet potato weevils; cracked tubers occur in compacted or stony soil.

hidroponik.tips: Sweet potato is a tuber crop that needs deep, loose soil for its roots to swell properly. Standard hydroponic systems cannot support healthy tuber development, so it is best grown in soil or large grow bags.

toxicity.note: Sweet potato is non-toxic to cats and dogs and is even used in some pet foods.

name: Pumpkin
description: A vigorous trailing vine that produces large, sweet-fleshed fruits; it needs plenty of space but rewards growers with long-storing harvests.

careDetails.watering: Water deeply once a day at the base; avoid wetting the leaves to prevent mildew.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with compost early, then switch to a phosphorus- and potassium-rich fertilizer once flowering begins.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch the growing tips after a few fruits have set so the plant feeds the existing pumpkins.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew on leaves and poor fruit set from a lack of pollinators are the most common issues.

hidroponik.tips: Pumpkin is a large sprawling vine with heavy fruits and an extensive root system. Standard hydroponic setups cannot support its size or the weight of its fruit, so it should be grown in the ground or very large containers.

toxicity.note: Pumpkin flesh is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs; plain cooked pumpkin is often given to aid digestion.

name: Sweet Corn
description: A tall cereal grass grown for its sweet, juicy kernels; best planted in blocks so the wind can pollinate the ears properly.

careDetails.watering: Water daily, especially during tasseling and ear filling when water demand peaks.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 8 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Corn is a heavy feeder — apply nitrogen-rich fertilizer at planting and again when knee-high.
careDetails.pruning: No pruning needed; simply remove dry lower leaves and keep the base weed-free.
careDetails.commonProblems: Corn earworm attacks the ear tips, and incomplete pollination leaves gaps of missing kernels.

hidroponik.tips: Sweet corn grows very tall and relies on wind pollination across a block of plants, plus it has a deep, demanding root system. These traits make it impractical for standard hydroponic systems, so grow it in soil with several rows planted together.

toxicity.note: The kernels are non-toxic, but never give the cob — corn cobs can cause dangerous intestinal blockages in dogs.

name: Leek
description: A mild, onion-family vegetable grown for its long white shank; tall and slender, it thrives in cool conditions and rich soil.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist; leeks dislike drying out.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a balanced nitrogen-rich fertilizer every two weeks for steady leafy growth.
careDetails.pruning: Trim the outer leaf tips if they yellow; mound soil around the base to blanch a longer white stem.
careDetails.commonProblems: Onion thrips and leek rust (orange leaf spots) are the most frequent troubles.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 70–90 days from transplant — harvest once the white shank is pencil-thick or larger
hidroponik.tips: Leek adapts well to hydroponics. To get a long, tender white shank without soil, grow it in tall net pots or wrap the lower stem in dark sleeves to blanch it. Sow seeds in rockwool first, then transplant.

toxicity.note: Toxic to cats and dogs — as an allium, leek damages red blood cells and can cause anemia even in small amounts.

name: Shallot
description: An aromatic bulbing onion that multiplies into clusters; a kitchen essential in Indonesian cooking, grown from small mother bulbs.

careDetails.watering: Water once a day in the morning; cut back sharply as the bulbs mature and tops fall over.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Use a potassium- and phosphorus-rich fertilizer to encourage firm bulb formation.
careDetails.pruning: No pruning needed; remove only dry or diseased leaves.
careDetails.commonProblems: Onion thrips and bulb rot from overly wet soil are common, especially in the rainy season.

hidroponik.tips: Shallot is a bulb crop, and bulbing alliums need to swell and cure in a firm, well-drained medium. The constant moisture of standard hydroponics tends to rot the bulbs rather than firm them up, so grow shallots in soil instead.

toxicity.note: Toxic to cats and dogs — shallots are alliums and are especially potent, damaging red blood cells and causing anemia.

name: Yardlong Bean
description: A fast-growing climbing legume famous for its extremely long, slender pods; productive in the heat and a staple of Indonesian gardens.

careDetails.watering: Water once a day; keep the soil evenly moist during flowering and pod set.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Being a legume it fixes its own nitrogen, so favor phosphorus and potassium for heavier podding.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch the main tip once it reaches the top of the trellis to push out more side-shoot pods.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids and pod borers attack the pods; mosaic virus can mottle and curl the leaves.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1200–1800 ppm (EC 2.4–3.6) — raise levels once pods set
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 45–60 days from sowing — pick pods young and tender every 2–3 days to keep them coming
hidroponik.tips: Yardlong bean does well in a Dutch bucket system but absolutely needs a sturdy trellis since the vines climb aggressively. Harvest the pods while still slim and snappy; leaving them too long makes them tough and slows new pods.

toxicity.note: Cooked pods are generally safe, but raw beans contain lectins that can upset a pet's stomach — never feed raw.

name: Curry Leaf
description: An aromatic small tree whose pungent leaves are essential to South Asian curries and tempering.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days, keeping the soil lightly moist but never waterlogged.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 5-6 hours of direct light daily.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with balanced fertilizer or compost monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch growing tips regularly to encourage a bushy, leafy habit.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf drop in cold weather and scale or aphid infestations.

hidroponik.tips: Curry leaf is a woody tree with a deep taproot and long-term growth that needs soil to develop properly, so hydroponic systems are not suitable for it.

toxicity.note: Curry leaf is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Bay Laurel
description: An evergreen shrub whose glossy aromatic leaves flavor soups, stews, and braises.

careDetails.watering: Water every 4-5 days; let the top of the soil dry between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; tolerates a range of light levels.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a slow-release fertilizer every 6 weeks during growth.
careDetails.pruning: Shape lightly and harvest mature leaves; bay tolerates pruning well.
careDetails.commonProblems: Slow growth and scale insects on the undersides of leaves.

hidroponik.tips: Bay laurel is a slow-growing woody shrub that develops an extensive root system over years and needs soil to thrive, so it is not suited to hydroponic cultivation.

toxicity.note: Toxic to pets — essential oils (eugenol) can cause vomiting and diarrhea in cats and dogs.

name: Lemon Verbena
description: A deciduous shrub with intensely lemon-scented leaves used in teas, desserts, and aromatherapy.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days, keeping the soil evenly moist during active growth.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours of direct light for the strongest aroma.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a light liquid fertilizer every month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune back regularly to keep it bushy and harvest fragrant leaf tips.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf drop after sudden cold and spider mites in dry indoor air.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 50-70 days — harvest leafy tips regularly once stems are established.
hidroponik.tips: Lemon verbena grows fast and leggy, so pinch tips often to keep it compact. Strong light keeps the lemon oils concentrated.

toxicity.note: Lemon verbena is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Stevia
description: A tender herb whose leaves are intensely sweet, used as a natural zero-calorie sugar substitute.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; stevia likes consistent moisture but not soggy roots.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to bright light, at least 6 hours daily.
careDetails.fertilizer: Use a light, low-nitrogen fertilizer monthly to avoid bitter leaves.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch off flower buds and trim tips to keep leaves sweet and plentiful.
careDetails.commonProblems: Reduced sweetness after flowering and root rot from overwatering.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Light herbal AB Mix 600-900 ppm (EC 1.2-1.8) — keep nitrogen modest
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 40-60 days — harvest leaves before flowering for peak sweetness.
hidroponik.tips: Stevia thrives in hydroponics with warm temperatures and strong light. Pinch flower buds early, since flowering lowers the sweetness of the leaves.

toxicity.note: Stevia is non-toxic and generally safe for cats and dogs.

name: Winter Savory
description: A hardy semi-evergreen herb with peppery leaves prized for seasoning beans and meats.

careDetails.watering: Water every 3-4 days; let the soil dry slightly between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours daily for the best flavor.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed lightly every 6 weeks; this herb prefers lean soil.
careDetails.pruning: Trim regularly to keep it compact and prevent woody, leggy stems.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot in heavy wet soil and stems turning woody with age.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700-1000 ppm (EC 1.4-2.0) — keep it moderate for strong flavor
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 50-70 days — snip 5-10 cm tips regularly once established.
hidroponik.tips: Winter savory dislikes soggy roots, so ensure excellent aeration in the nutrient solution. Keeping nutrients moderate yields a more pungent, peppery leaf.

toxicity.note: Winter savory is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Lovage
description: A tall perennial herb with a bold celery-like flavor used in soups, stocks, and salads.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; lovage likes consistently moist, rich soil.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; tolerates light shade well.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a nitrogen-rich fertilizer monthly for lush leafy growth.
careDetails.pruning: Cut back stems to encourage tender new leaves and remove flower stalks.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids on new shoots and bitter leaves once the plant flowers.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 800-1100 ppm (EC 1.6-2.2) — it is a heavier feeder
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 50-70 days — harvest outer leaves first and pick before flowering.
hidroponik.tips: Lovage grows tall, so give it a deep system and support if needed. Harvest young leaves often, since older leaves and flowering turn the flavor harsh.

toxicity.note: Use with caution — contains furanocoumarins that can cause photosensitivity; large amounts may upset a pet's stomach.

name: Tarragon
description: A slender perennial herb with an anise-like flavor central to French cuisine and sauces.

careDetails.watering: Water every 3 days; tarragon dislikes wet feet, so let soil dry slightly.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours daily for the best aroma.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed lightly every 6 weeks; too much fertilizer weakens the flavor.
careDetails.pruning: Trim regularly to encourage fresh tender growth and prevent legginess.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot in heavy soil and weak flavor in low light.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700-1000 ppm (EC 1.4-2.0) — keep moderate to preserve aroma
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 50-70 days — snip leafy tips regularly once the plant is established.
hidroponik.tips: French tarragon does not set viable seed, so start from cuttings. Provide strong aeration and moderate nutrients, since excess feeding dilutes its anise flavor.

toxicity.note: Use with caution — contains estragole; large amounts may cause stomach upset in cats and dogs.

name: Holy Basil (Tulsi)
description: A sacred aromatic basil revered in Ayurveda, used in teas and herbal remedies.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; keep soil evenly moist but well drained.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours of direct light daily.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a balanced liquid fertilizer every 2-3 weeks during growth.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch flower spikes and tips often to keep leaves abundant and aromatic.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids and whiteflies, plus rapid flowering that reduces leaf quality.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 40-60 days — harvest leafy tips regularly and pinch flowers early.
hidroponik.tips: Holy basil is one of the easiest herbs for hydroponics and grows vigorously with warmth and strong light. Pinch flower spikes early to keep the leaves tender and aromatic.

toxicity.note: Holy basil is non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs.

name: Ylang-Ylang
description: A tropical tree whose intensely sweet, golden flowers yield one of the most prized essential oils in perfumery.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist but never waterlogged; water every 2-3 days in warm weather.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; a young tree appreciates protection from harsh midday sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed monthly during the growing season with a balanced fertilizer to support abundant flowering.
careDetails.pruning: Prune to control height and encourage a bushier canopy, since the tree can grow very tall.
careDetails.commonProblems: Sensitive to cold and wind; leaves may drop if temperatures fall below ideal tropical conditions.

hidroponik.tips: Ylang-ylang is a large woody tree with a deep, vigorous root system, so it is not suited to hydroponics. Grow it in the ground or a very large pot with rich, well-draining soil.

toxicity.note: The essential oil can cause skin irritation and lethargy in pets; keep concentrated oil away from animals.

name: Patchouli
description: A leafy tropical herb famous for its deep, earthy, musky scent widely used in perfumes and incense.

careDetails.watering: Likes consistently moist soil; water every 1-2 days and never let it dry out completely.
careDetails.sunlight: Partial shade to filtered light; intense direct sun can scorch the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a nitrogen-rich liquid fertilizer monthly to promote lush leaf growth.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch the growing tips regularly to keep the plant bushy and encourage fragrant new foliage.
careDetails.commonProblems: Wilts quickly if underwatered; prone to fungal leaf spots in stagnant, overly humid air.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 800–1200 ppm (EC 1.6–2.4) — patchouli is a hungry leafy herb
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 70–90 days — harvest the upper leaves once the plant is well established
hidroponik.tips: Patchouli thrives in hydroponics thanks to its love of constant moisture. Keep the solution well-oxygenated and the nutrient temperature cool to avoid root rot in its soft stems.

toxicity.note: Generally low risk, but the concentrated essential oil may cause stomach upset; avoid letting pets ingest the oil.

name: Vetiver
description: A tall perennial grass whose fragrant roots produce a smoky, woody essential oil prized for grounding aromatherapy.

careDetails.watering: Very adaptable; water every 3-4 days, though established clumps tolerate both drought and brief flooding.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun is best for vigorous growth and the strongest root fragrance.
careDetails.fertilizer: Undemanding; a light feeding every 2 months is plenty for this hardy grass.
careDetails.pruning: Trim the foliage back periodically to keep clumps tidy and channel energy into the roots.
careDetails.commonProblems: Almost trouble-free; its main drawback is becoming a dense, hard-to-divide clump over time.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (expanded clay) in a net pot
hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix 700–1100 ppm (EC 1.4–2.2) — a grass that handles a fairly wide nutrient range
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 8–12 months — roots are harvested once they have developed a strong aroma
hidroponik.tips: Vetiver is naturally semi-aquatic, so it adapts beautifully to deep water culture. Give the roots plenty of room and strong aeration, as the prized oil concentrates in a well-developed root mass.

toxicity.note: Vetiver is non-toxic and safe around pets.

name: Tuberose
description: A bulb-grown flower with waxy white blooms famous for an intoxicating night-time fragrance used in fine perfumery.

careDetails.watering: Water regularly while growing and flowering, but reduce sharply once the bulbs go dormant.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun for at least 6 hours daily to fuel strong stalks and abundant blooms.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with a phosphorus-rich fertilizer monthly during active growth to boost flowering.
careDetails.pruning: Cut spent flower spikes after blooming so the bulb can store energy for next season.
careDetails.commonProblems: Bulbs rot easily in cold, wet soil; they may also skip flowering if planted too shallow.

hidroponik.tips: Tuberose grows from a storage bulb that needs a dry dormancy period and is highly prone to rot when kept constantly wet, so hydroponics is unsuitable. Plant the bulbs in free-draining soil instead.

toxicity.note: Best kept away from pets; ingestion of the bulbs or sap may cause mild digestive upset.

name: Gardenia
description: An evergreen shrub with glossy leaves and creamy white flowers prized for their rich, sweet jasmine-like perfume.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil evenly moist with soft, lime-free water; let the top layer dry slightly between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light with some morning sun; protect from harsh afternoon sun that scorches buds.
careDetails.fertilizer: Feed with an acidic fertilizer for ericaceous plants every month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune lightly right after flowering to shape the shrub and remove spent blooms.
careDetails.commonProblems: Notorious for bud drop and yellowing leaves (chlorosis) when soil pH, watering, or light is even slightly off.

hidroponik.tips: Gardenia is a woody, acid-loving shrub with a fussy root system that resents the constantly wet roots of hydroponics. Grow it in acidic, well-draining potting mix where its pH can be carefully managed.

toxicity.note: Toxic to pets — ingestion can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and hives.

name: Curry Plant
description: A silvery Mediterranean herb, also called immortelle, whose foliage smells strongly of curry and yields a healing essential oil.

careDetails.watering: Drought-tolerant; water sparingly and let the soil dry out well between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day brings out the strongest aroma and the best silvery foliage.
careDetails.fertilizer: Needs very little feeding; a light dose every 2 months suits this lean-soil herb.
careDetails.pruning: Trim after flowering to keep the plant compact and prevent it from becoming woody and leggy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot from overwatering or heavy soil is the main risk in humid tropical climates.

hidroponik.media: Coarse perlite or rockwool with excellent drainage
hidroponik.nutrisi: Diluted herbal AB Mix 600–900 ppm (EC 1.2–1.8) — keep it lean to preserve the aroma
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days — harvest fragrant sprigs once the plant is well established
hidroponik.tips: As a Mediterranean herb, Helichrysum hates wet feet, so use a low-volume system like NFT or drip with strong aeration. Keep nutrients lean and let roots breathe to avoid rot and concentrate the oils.

toxicity.note: Generally low risk, but the concentrated essential oil may cause irritation or upset if ingested by pets.

name: Moringa
description: A fast-growing 'miracle tree' whose leaves are packed with vitamins, protein, and antioxidants, widely used to support nutrition and overall wellness.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2–3 days; tolerant of drought once established, so avoid waterlogging.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun all day for the best leaf production.
careDetails.fertilizer: Compost or manure every 2 months; it grows well even in poor soil.
careDetails.pruning: Cut back the main stem regularly to keep the tree short and bushy for easy leaf harvesting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot in waterlogged soil and occasional caterpillars on young leaves.

hidroponik.tips: Moringa is a deep-rooted tree that can reach 3–10 meters with a strong taproot, so it is not suited to home hydroponic systems and is best grown in the ground.

toxicity.note: Leaves are non-toxic and nutritious; the root and bark contain alkaloids — use with caution.

name: Neem
description: A hardy evergreen tree prized for its bitter leaves and oil, traditionally used as a natural antiseptic, insect repellent, and skin remedy.

careDetails.watering: Water every 3–4 days when young; mature trees are very drought tolerant.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun; neem thrives in hot, dry tropical climates.
careDetails.fertilizer: Compost every 3 months; it tolerates poor, sandy soils well.
careDetails.pruning: Prune to shape the canopy and remove dead branches once a season.
careDetails.commonProblems: Scale insects and root rot if the soil stays soggy.

hidroponik.tips: Neem is a large tree that can grow 15–20 meters tall with an extensive deep root system, making it unsuitable for hydroponics; grow it in well-drained ground instead.

toxicity.note: Neem oil and seeds (azadirachtin) can cause vomiting and lethargy if ingested by pets — use with caution.

name: Bitter Melon
description: A climbing vine bearing warty, intensely bitter fruit that is widely used to help manage blood sugar and support digestion.

careDetails.watering: Water daily and keep the soil consistently moist, especially while fruiting.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours a day.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK every 2 weeks, higher potassium once fruiting begins.
careDetails.pruning: Train the vine onto a trellis and pinch tips to encourage side branches and more fruit.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies and powdery mildew in humid conditions.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1500–2500 ppm (EC 3.0–5.0) — raise potassium during fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 55–70 days from sowing — harvest fruit while still firm and light green
hidroponik.tips: Bitter melon grows vigorously in a Dutch Bucket system but needs a sturdy trellis for its vines. Provide strong support and good aeration of the nutrient solution with an air pump.

toxicity.note: The seeds and red arils are toxic and the fruit is very bitter; can cause vomiting and diarrhea in pets — use with caution.

name: Roselle
description: A shrubby hibiscus whose tart red calyces are brewed into a vitamin C-rich tea valued for supporting blood pressure and heart health.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; keep moist during flowering and calyx formation.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun for abundant flowering and deep-red calyces.
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer monthly; reduce nitrogen as flowering nears.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch growing tips early to make the plant bushier and more productive.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids and leaf-eating beetles on young foliage.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 120–150 days from sowing — harvest the fleshy calyces about 2–3 weeks after flowering
hidroponik.tips: Roselle is a tall shrub, so use a large-volume Dutch Bucket system and support the stems. Plenty of light during flowering produces the deepest-colored, most flavorful calyces.

toxicity.note: Roselle is generally non-toxic to cats and dogs and is widely consumed as a tea.

name: Insulin Plant
description: A spiral-stemmed herb whose leaves are traditionally chewed to help lower blood sugar, giving it the popular name 'insulin plant'.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; it likes consistently moist but well-drained soil.
careDetails.sunlight: Partial shade to bright indirect light; protect from harsh midday sun.
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic liquid fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.pruning: Remove old or yellowing leaves and divide clumps when crowded.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf-tip browning from dry air and root rot if overwatered.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days from rooting — harvest mature leaves continuously once established
hidroponik.tips: The insulin plant roots easily from stem cuttings placed in water, making it well suited to a simple Kratky setup. Keep it out of direct hot sun so the leaves stay broad and green.

toxicity.note: Pet safety data is limited; as a medicinal blood-sugar-lowering herb, give only with caution.

name: Toothache Plant
description: A low spreading herb with yellow-and-red 'eyeball' flower heads that produce a tingling, numbing sensation, traditionally used to relieve toothache and mouth sores.

careDetails.watering: Water daily; it likes steadily moist soil and wilts quickly if it dries out.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun to partial shade; full sun gives the strongest flowering.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light liquid fertilizer every 3 weeks.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch off spent flower heads to keep new blooms coming.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids on young shoots and root rot in waterlogged soil.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 40–60 days from sowing — harvest leaves and flower buds, which carry the strongest tingling effect
hidroponik.tips: This compact herb grows fast and is easy in a Kratky or NFT system. Harvest flower buds for the strongest spilanthol (numbing compound) content and pick regularly to encourage bushy growth.

toxicity.note: Contains spilanthol, which causes a numbing, tingling sensation in the mouth; give only with caution to pets.
